name: random-sampling-eval
description: Evaluate the personality analysis model's accuracy by scoring N=100 random Bilibili users and comparing against DeepSeek annotator consensus. Produces AUC-ROC, per-axis calibration, and stratified breakdowns.

steps.title: Generate and validate random UID sample
steps.change: Generate 100 random UIDs in range 1–700M. Use AICU API or bilibiliCrawler to check each UID has ≥10 public comments. Replace UIDs with <10 comments by resampling. Output: .claude/random_sampling_eval/sample_uids.json
steps.verify: File sample_uids.json exists with 100 UIDs, each validated to have ≥10 comments

steps.title: Scrape full comment history for each sampled UID
steps.change: For each UID in sample, fetch comments + danmaku via POST /api/aicu/scrape (up to 10 pages each). Checkpoint after each UID so interruption loses zero progress. Output: .claude/random_sampling_eval/user_data/{uid}.json per user
steps.verify: 100 JSON files exist in user_data/, each with comments[] and danmaku[] arrays

steps.title: Run headless scoring pipeline on each user
steps.change: Extract scoreComments() from src/main.jsx into a headless scoring module OR call via API endpoint. For each user: load keyword dictionary → findLexiconMarks → classifySpeechAct → blend hybrid scores → output troll_index + 4-axis radar. Output: .claude/random_sampling_eval/scored/{uid}.json per user
steps.verify: 100 JSON files in scored/ with troll_index (0-100) and axis scores (toxicEmotions, missingCommitment, missingIntelligibility, otherReasons)

steps.title: Annotate user profiles with DeepSeek A1/A2/A3 consensus
steps.change: For each user, run annotateLabelsWithDeepSeek.js with A1 (balanced), A2 (strict), A3 (consensus) personas on the user's full comment set. Each annotator rates the 4 Ziegenbein axes (0-2). Compute majority-vote consensus label per axis. Derive binary ground-truth: consensus_avg ≥ 1.0 → argumentative. Output: .claude/random_sampling_eval/annotated/{uid}.json per user
steps.verify: 100 JSON files in annotated/ with A1/A2/A3 ratings, consensus per axis, and binary label

steps.title: Compute evaluation metrics
steps.change: Load all 100 scored + annotated results. Compute: (a) AUC-ROC for troll_index vs binary label, (b) precision/recall/F1 at threshold 50, (c) per-axis Brier score and ECE (via calibration.py), (d) bootstrap 95% CIs on all metrics (N=1000 resamples), (e) stratified breakdowns by comment volume tercile. Reuse python_backend/analysis/validation_metrics.py and calibration.py where possible. Output: .claude/random_sampling_eval/metrics.json
steps.verify: All metrics computed without errors. AUC-ROC > 0.5 (better than random). Bootstrap CIs are finite.

steps.title: Generate evaluation report
steps.change: Write .claude/random_sampling_eval/report.md with: executive summary (is the model useful?), per-axis reliability table (AUC, Brier, F1), calibration chart descriptions, stratified breakdown by comment volume, limitations (DeepSeek-as-ground-truth circularity risk, no human labels for whole-user profiles), and recommendations for threshold tuning or weight adjustment.
steps.verify: report.md exists with all sections and actionable recommendations

notes.ground_truth_gap: No human-labeled whole-user profiles exist. DeepSeek A1/A2/A3 annotator consensus serves as proxy ground truth. This introduces circularity risk (DeepSeek is both the scoring model AND the annotator). Mitigation: use different prompt personas for annotation vs scoring, and treat results as IRR validation rather than external accuracy.
notes.statistical_power: N=100 users provides ~80% power to detect AUC ≥ 0.65 vs null hypothesis of 0.5 (α=0.05, two-sided). For per-axis calibration, 100 users with 4 axes = 400 observation points.
notes.rate_limiting: AICU API has no documented rate limit but scraping 100 users × 10 pages is ~1000 HTTP requests. Budget ~30 minutes with conservative pacing (2s delay between requests).
